profile.profession: Medical student preparing for NEET PG
profile.personality: earnest, slightly anxious about exams, opens up about study stress

transcript.content: Hi Aarti, I'm Miss Maya. It's a warm evening, I just thought of how lovely chai feels with a rainy monsoon. How was your day?
Hi Maya, nice to meet you. My day was okay, busy with studies. NEET PG prep is tough, but I manage. Sometimes I feel overwhelmed, but I try to stay focused. Did you have tea today?
Tea is part of my evening! NEET PG prep sounds intense — what part feels most overwhelming right now?

The clinical subjects are the most confusing — like pharmacology and pathology. Sometimes I feel like I’m memorizing but not really understanding. Also, the pressure to score well is stressing me out a bit. कभी-कभी मुझे सोचते हैं, "क्या मैं यह सब कर सकती हूँ?

You're doing great, just a tiny tweak — we say "I wonder if I can do all this." The doubt is real when you're juggling so much. What helps you feel more solid with the clinical subjects — practice, discussions, or something else?

I try to make flashcards and revise daily, but it’s still hard. Maybe I need to do more case-based practice. My friend and I sometimes discuss tough topics — that helps a bit. But I get distracted easily. How do you stay focused?
You're doing the basics right ,  flashcards and revision. Distracting is normal, but practice makes it easier. What one small habit could help you stay more focused today?

Maybe I can try studying in shorter time slots — like 25 minutes, then a break. I’ve heard of the Pomodoro method. Also, turning off my phone helps, but I still check messages sometimes. Hmm... I should try that today. Thanks, Maya. It was good talking. I have to go now.
You're welcome, Aarti — small steps make a big difference. Tell me how the Pomodoro method works out for you. Take care and talk soon!
Sure, Maya. I'll give it a try. I think short study times might help me focus better. Thanks again for the advice. Take care! 😊
You're welcome, short bursts can really help. Tell me how it goes. Take care and talk soon!
